originalTitle: Why Ukrainian strikes on annexed Crimea are a blow to Putin
summary: Eastern Europe correspondent Sarah Rainsford explains why Crimea is a target for Kyiv, and why the peninsula matters to Vladimir Putin.
content:
A resident inside Crimea has told the BBC the situation there is "catastrophic", as Ukrainian drones strike oil refineries and power plants on the peninsula, as well as military targets.

The strikes on Russian-annexed Crimea have increased significantly in recent weeks - partly to cut off supply lines to Russian troops attacking Ukraine from the south.

Eastern Europe correspondent Sarah Rainsford explains why Kyiv is striking now and why the peninsula matters to Vladimir Putin personally.
chineseTitle: 为什么乌克兰对被吞并的克里米亚的袭击对普京来说是一个打击
chineseSummary: 东欧记者莎拉·雷恩斯福德解释了为什么克里米亚是基辅的目标，以及为什么该半岛对弗拉基米尔·普京很重要。
chineseContent:
A resident inside Crimea has told the BBC the situation there is "catastrophic", as Ukrainian drones strike oil refineries and power plants on the peninsula, as well as military targets.

最近几周，对俄罗斯吞并的克里米亚的袭击大幅增加，部分原因是为了切断从南部攻击乌克兰的俄罗斯军队的补给线。

东欧记者萨拉·雷恩斯福德 (Sarah Rainsford) 解释了为什么基辅现在正在罢工，以及为什么半岛对弗拉基米尔·普京个人很重要。
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
